subject: Biology
title: Whales: Giants of the Deep
description: A quick, practical guide to Whales: Giants of the Deep.

hook.text: The blue whale is the largest animal that has ever lived—bigger than any dinosaur.

buildup.text: A blue whale's heart is the size of a small car. Its tongue weighs as much as an elephant.

discovery.text: Despite their size, blue whales eat krill—shrimp-like creatures just 5 cm long—up to 4 tons daily.

twist.text: Whales evolved from small wolf-like land mammals that returned to the sea 50 million years ago.

climax.text: Whale poop fertilizes ocean surface waters, boosting phytoplankton growth and carbon capture.

punchline.text: The biggest animal eats the smallest food. And saves the planet doing it.
